title: mentha longifolia var. marisensis
intro: Mentha longifolia var. marisensis, commonly known as wild marjoram, is a variety of the perennial herb Mentha longifolia, native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues, ease respiratory problems, and promote relaxation, offering a range of health benefits. Wild marjoram can be consumed as a tea, used in herbal infusions, or added to topical creams and lotions, making it a versatile plant for herbal preparations.
